text.title: django-service-layer Failure Modes
text.summary: Five concrete antipatterns. Each maps to a rule in `01-core-rules.xml`.

antipattern.description: Business logic embedded in view methods (multi-model writes, side effects in the same function as request parsing).
antipattern.symptom: Service unit tests require mock HTTP requests; same logic copy-pasted into Celery task with subtle drift; view file grows past 300 lines.
antipattern.root-cause: "Just one quick endpoint" â€” no place to put logic exists yet, so it lives in the view.
antipattern.fix: Extract into `apps/<app>/services.py` with kwarg-only signature. Views become 3 lines: validate input via serializer, call service, wrap response.

antipattern.description: `Model.objects.create(...)` used for new instances whose model defines `clean()` or composite constraints.
antipattern.symptom: Invalid rows reach the database; downstream services blow up on missing or contradictory fields; integrity errors surface days later.
antipattern.root-cause: `objects.create` is a one-liner; `Model(...) + full_clean() + save()` is three lines. Convenience wins until incidents.
antipattern.fix: Replace with `instance = Model(...); instance.full_clean(); instance.save()`. Keep `objects.create` only for trivial inserts where `clean()` is empty.

antipattern.description: Service raises `rest_framework.exceptions.ValidationError`, `Http404`, or returns a DRF Response.
antipattern.symptom: Celery task / management command imports the service and crashes on import or fails with `ImproperlyConfigured` at runtime; the same business rule cannot be reused outside the web context.
antipattern.root-cause: Convenience again â€” the DRF exception "just works" in the view's exception handler.
antipattern.fix: Raise domain exceptions (`ApplicationError`, `NotFoundError`, `ValidationError`, `PermissionDeniedError`) defined in `core/exceptions.py`. Add a DRF custom exception handler that maps domain â†’ HTTP.

antipattern.symptom: Worker logs show "Order matching query does not exist" immediately after a creation endpoint â€” the task ran before the transaction committed.
antipattern.root-cause: Author treats `.delay()` as async-fire-and-forget; doesn't realise the broker accepts the message before the SQL commits.
antipattern.fix: Wrap every side-effect dispatch in `transaction.on_commit(lambda: task.delay(...))`. The lambda fires after commit and is dropped on rollback.

antipattern.description: Service writes to two or more models without a single enclosing `transaction.atomic()` block.
antipattern.symptom: Partial state: Order rows exist with no OrderItems; payment row created but order not; manual cleanup tickets pile up.
antipattern.root-cause: Author assumes Django default behaviour wraps everything; it does not (unless `ATOMIC_REQUESTS=True` is set, which is a different antipattern).
antipattern.fix: Open `with transaction.atomic():` at the service body. Verify rollback by deliberately raising mid-service in tests.
